2010-09-01.title: President Obama’s Address on Iraq
2010-09-01.abstract: The text, as prepared for delivery, of President Obama’s address from the Oval Office on Tuesday night, provided by the White House.

2010-09-01.title: After Years of War, Few Iraqis Have a Clear View of the Future
2010-09-01.abstract: Many of the same complaints about daily life are heard now as in 2003, when the United States invasion transformed the political life of the nation.

2010-09-01.abstract: President Obama is looking for simultaneous progress on Iraq, Iran and Israeli-Palestinian peace, a triple play that has eluded his predecessors for decades.

2010-09-01.title: A Fragile Turning Point
2010-09-01.abstract: The summer of 2006 felt like a fragile turning point, a tenuous peace that could easily be interrupted. The country today is at another turning point. And its peace may be no less fragile.

2010-09-02.title: Grief Across Latin America for Migrant Killings
2010-09-02.abstract: Migrants are apparently dying at the hands of a drug gang seeking money or possibly recruits, officials said, though no nobody knows for sure.

2010-09-02.title: Britain: Inquest Into Spy’s Death
2010-09-02.abstract: The body of a British spy, found dead in his apartment in London last month, had been padlocked into a gym bag and put in the bathtub, but how he died is not yet certain.

2010-09-02.title: South Africa: Sympathy Strike Is Suspended as Talks Continue
2010-09-02.abstract: Negotiations were continuing on the government’s offer to give more than a million public employees pay raises of 7.5 percent, double the inflation rate.

2010-09-02.title: In Somali Civil War, Both Sides Embrace Pirates
2010-09-02.abstract: After years of not getting sucked into the messy civil war on land, Somalia’s pirate gangs are taking sides — both sides.

2010-09-02.abstract: Middle East negotiators agreed to keep talking, but the issue of West Bank settlements threatens to derail the negotiations.

2010-09-03.abstract: Rwandan outrage over a report suggesting that its troops might have been responsible for genocide in Congo prompted the United Nations to delay the official release for a month.

2010-09-03.abstract: Some veteran peace process practitioners say Israel and the Palestinians should first aim for a partial solution because the gaps between the positions are too wide.

2010-09-04.abstract: Recent food riots in poor countries prompted United Nations officials to call for a careful watch on prices.

2010-09-29.title: In Multilingual Switzerland, One Tongue Struggles
2010-09-29.abstract: In one corner of the country, depending on whom you talk to, there is either strong support or bitter resistance to Romansh.

2010-09-29.title: France: Eiffel Tower Evacuated After Another Bomb Scare
2010-09-29.abstract: The Eiffel Tower was evacuated and closed for two hours after an anonymous call that a bomb was about to go off, the second such evacuation in a month.

2010-09-29.title: Ancient Italian Town Has Wind at Its Back
2010-09-29.abstract: Faced with high electricity rates, small communities across Italy are making renewable energy.

2010-09-29.title: Test of India Verdict Will Lie in Public Reaction
2010-09-29.abstract: A ruling is expected on Thursday over a religious site where Hindu-Muslim riots erupted in 1992, and the case has become a test of India’s secular soul.

2010-09-29.title: France: Parliament Debates Tougher Immigration Legislation
2010-09-29.abstract: An immigration bill under which people who have acquired French citizenship within the last 10 years would strip them of that citizenship if they committed certain violent crimes.

2010-09-30.title: Video Hints at Executions by Pakistanis
2010-09-30.abstract: A video of executions by uniformed men sparked concerns over killings by Pakistani soldiers, who have U.S. support.

2010-09-30.title: In a Computer Worm, a Possible Biblical Clue
2010-09-30.abstract: A reference in computer code to the Book of Esther may or may not be a sign of an Israeli origin to the Stuxnet worm.

2010-09-30.title: Web Tastes Freedom Inside Syria, and It’s Bitter
2010-09-30.abstract: A draft law regulating online media would clamp down on bloggers and other journalists.

2010-09-30.title: Talk of Reform to Enliven Leaders’ Meeting in China
2010-09-30.abstract: Some officials have been calling for an overhaul of the country’s political system, while others have explicitly rejected such talk.

2010-09-30.title: Trying to Lace Together a Consensus on Biodiversity Across a Global Landscape
2010-09-30.abstract: Despite about two decades of conferences, conventions and commitments, the earth’s biodiversity is steadily eroding as ecosystems suffer and species die out.
